summary: Backend + frontend smoke and integration tests for Once Were Wild. 11/11 backend pytest cases pass; all six core UI flows (Home, Pricing, Gallery+Lightbox, Contact validation+submit, Footer quick-enquiry, Admin login -> MFA) pass. One important deployment issue identified: brute-force lockout never triggers via the public preview URL because request.client.host returns the k8s ingress proxy IP, which rotates per request.

critical.issue: Brute-force lockout never triggers when called through the public ingress URL. server.py uses request.client.host to build the rate-limit identifier (ip+email), but behind the k8s ingress this returns the proxy/load-balancer IP which rotates per request (observed 10.208.138.130, 10.208.138.133, etc). Result: each failed attempt creates a new identifier with count=1, so the 3-strike lockout is effectively bypassed from any real client. Confirmed working on http://localhost:8001 directly. FIX: derive client IP from X-Forwarded-For (first hop) or use only the email as the identifier.

minor.issue: On the 3rd failed attempt the lockout record is written with count=0 (reset) which is unusual. Subsequent attempts return 429 correctly because of locked_until, but the count field reset makes attempt tracking opaque. Cosmetic only.

issues: data-testid naming inconsistency: the contact info <li> blocks use testids 'contact-email', 'contact-phone' which collide conceptually with the form input testids 'contact-email-input', 'contact-phone-input'. The task spec referred to 'contact-email' as the form field testid. Recommend renaming the <li> testids to 'contact-info-email', 'contact-info-phone', etc., to avoid future automation confusion.

action_items: Fix lockout: read client IP from X-Forwarded-For in /api/auth/login (or key the rate limit on email only). Today the 3-strike 30-min lockout is silently disabled in the deployed environment.
Optional: rename Contact info-block testids to 'contact-info-*' to remove naming overlap with form inputs.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py login uses request.client.host without consulting X-Forwarded-For — broken behind reverse proxy / k8s ingress; security control bypassed.
Login lockout zeroes 'count' on lock; consider keeping count for forensics.
Mongo _id is correctly excluded in all list endpoints via projection {'_id': 0} — good.
All admin endpoints depend on get_current_admin (Bearer) — 401 behaviour validated.
Pydantic validation + custom 422 message for short contact messages works as expected.
MFA setup flow correctly returns secret only the first time and persists mfa_secret on first successful verify.
Settings PUT is upsert + GET reflects change — verified end-to-end.
server.py is 396 lines (single file) — within limits but approaching the soft threshold; consider splitting into routers (auth, admin, contact, media, settings) for maintainability.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (6/6 flows: Home, Pricing, Gallery+Lightbox, Contact, Footer quick-enquiry, Admin login -> MFA)

test_credentials: info@oncewerewild.com / WildAtHeart2026. MFA secret retrieved from POST /api/auth/login (status mfa_setup) on first login; on subsequent logins read users.mfa_secret from Mongo or scan QR.
seed_data_creation: Test data uses TEST_ prefix in emails, captions, alt_text. Created (and cleaned up) media items, settings updates restored, contact submissions left in DB (admin can use them).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin user already has MFA enabled (mfa_enabled=true) after first login during this test run. Therefore /api/auth/login will now return status=mfa_required (no secret in response). The pytest fixture reads users.mfa_secret directly from Mongo to compute TOTP for subsequent runs — keep using that pattern. To force the mfa_setup path again, unset mfa_enabled/mfa_secret on the users doc. login_attempts collection is cleared at session start and end. The frontend admin route is /admin and reaches mfa-verify (or mfa-setup) after correct email+password.
rca of the issue: Brute-force lockout RCA: server.py line 142 uses ip = request.client.host. Behind the Emergent k8s ingress, FastAPI/Uvicorn sees the proxy hop IP, which is not sticky per client — observed values 10.208.138.130 and 10.208.138.133 across consecutive requests from the same test runner. The lockout identifier is therefore different on each request, so the count never reaches the 3-threshold for a real attacker. Reproduction: POST /api/auth/login with bad password 4x from a single client through the preview URL — all 4 return 401, and db.login_attempts shows two separate identifiers each at count=2. Direct localhost:8001 hits do trigger 429 on the 3rd attempt as designed. Mitigation: use the leftmost public IP from X-Forwarded-For (the ingress sets it), or key the lockout on email only (simpler, but enables target lockout by an attacker — accept that risk or combine email-only lockout with a per-IP captcha).
